headline: NHTSA Closed Two Probes Covering 817,000 Vehicles in One Weekend. Nobody Checked If the Fixes Work.

critic_notes.general_editor: Strong lede, clear case→system structure, good pull stat. At 1005w exceeds 300-500 target but justified by required Limitations/Counterargument/Actionable sections.
critic_notes.voice_coach: Zero em dashes/banned phrases/The-starters. Rhythm PASS (227.9). Good fragment diversity. 'Paperwork cleared.' slightly forced but voice is distinct.
critic_notes.ethics_reviewer: Structural critique without preaching. Counterargument genuinely strong. Martha Avila named respectfully. No moral superiority.
critic_notes.social_shareability: Headline is viral-ready. 96% pull stat perfect share trigger. Time asymmetry (617 days open, weekend to close) is the anchor.
critic_notes.legal_accuracy: 5 refs all properly cited. Tesla software version 2023.38.4 should be verified. Katy TX crash sourced from Memeburn not primary.
critic_notes.research_rigor: Novel contribution (time asymmetry + missing residual-risk metric) is clear. OIG data is 2018-2019 vintage, acknowledged. Could estimate unfixed count (817K×30%≈245K).
critic_notes.data_presentation: Numbers clean and well-layered. 70% figure used twice (body + structural) is minor redundancy. Could calculate the 245K unfixed estimate.
